items.title: Sanofi defends Dupixent patents, but execs insist it has nothing to do with CEO change
items.snippet: Sanofi’s interim leadership sought on a Thursday earnings call to quell concerns that its sudden defense of Dupixent’s patents had anything to do with the departure of CEO Paul Hudson.

items.title: Unnamed pharma files citizen petition targeting FDA’s trove of rejection letters
items.snippet: The FDA in July 2025 made publicly available over 200 complete response letters—an initiative that the investment community sees as “unanimously positive,” analysts told BioSpace.

items.title: Roche insists amylin obesity drug still valuable for patients who ‘don’t want side effects’
items.snippet: Roche and Zealand Pharma announced last month that their amylin analog petrelintide elicited a 9% placebo-controlled weight reduction at 42 weeks—falling far below analyst and investor expectations.

items.title: AAN 2026: J&J, Kyverna, Capricor and Praxis showcase practice-changing data
items.snippet: This year’s American Academy of Neurology meeting included a presentation that could one day set a new treatment standard for myasthenia gravis.

items.title: AbbVie commits $1.4B to NC plant in its largest-ever single campus investment
items.snippet: The Durham plant will manufacture immunology, neuroscience and oncology drugs once AbbVie finishes construction in 2028.

items.title: The next era of diabetes management
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you'll be hearing from Dr. Sarah Howell, CEO at Arecor Therapeutics and Dr. Wendy S. Lane, clinical endocrinologist and diabetologist. We examine how increasingly connected and tailored diabetes technologies are reframing the field’s central opportunity around minimizing the day-to-day de...

items.title: Life sciences still falls short on pay equity
items.snippet: Women and nonwhite racial/ethnic groups are still earning less in the life sciences. An industry consultant discusses the pay disparities, ways to fix the gaps and why change is needed.

items.title: 11 companies hiring in Illinois now
items.snippet: Looking for a biopharma job in Illinois? Check out the BioSpace list of 11 companies hiring life sciences professionals like you.

items.title: Washington’s life sciences jobs continue increasing, up 50% from 2014-2024: report
items.snippet: While life sciences employment was down 0.3% nationally in 2024, it rose 1.9% in Washington, according to a new Life Science Washington report. The association’s CEO discusses the latest findings and how the state’s job market looks this year.

items.title: FDA issues warning letter after GLP-1 manufacturer refuses inspectors access
items.snippet: New Life told FDA inspectors that they lacked the authority to enter parts of a facility where it made the GLP-1 receptor agonists semaglutide and tirzepatide.

items.title: Trump’s psychedelics push a mixed blessing for neuropsychiatric companies, patients
items.snippet: With a greenlight for ibogaine to enter clinical testing and three unnamed products set to receive Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers this week, it’s full speed ahead for psychedelics. But will sidestepping normal regulatory protocols actually be a net negative for the field?

items.title: The 4 biggest hiring mistakes biopharma executives keep making
items.snippet: Finding the right people for critical open roles can be difficult even for biopharma leaders. In this column, Kaye/Bassman’s Michael Pietrack discusses four pitfalls executives face during the hiring process, starting with confusing scientific brilliance with leadership ability.

items.title: Replimune lays off over 200 in Massachusetts
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Ionis’ antisense drug stabilizes ultra-rare disease ahead of FDA decision
items.snippet: The pivotal study of zilganersen in Alexander disease missed a secondary endpoint, but analysts expect the FDA to approve the asset given the unmet need and overall data.

items.title: Lilly exits Rigel alliance, adding to RIPK1 scrap heap
items.snippet: Eli Lilly and Rigel Pharmaceuticals partnered in February 2021 to advance a pair of RIPK1 blockers, but the pharma in October last year pulled the plug on one of these programs for central nervous system indications.

items.title: Trump’s psychedelics push, Lilly’s up to $7B CAR T deal, Replimune’s saga and Denali’s big win
items.snippet: The deals keep rolling in, with Lilly penning a $7 billion pact for gene delivery biotech Kelonia Therapeutics and UCB taking over cell therapy-focused Neurona Therapeutics; President Trump signed a new executive order supporting the development of psychedelic therapies, sparking fanfare and concern alike; and the FDA...

items.title: Lilly, Novo dip as proposed Medicare coverage for GLP-1 pilot thrown off balance
items.snippet: Two of the biggest insurance providers have expressed reluctance to participate in the government’s BALANCE program that would have made GLP-1 drugs more affordable to patients.

items.title: Amid US ordeal, Moderna wins EU approval for flu/COVID-19 combo shot
items.snippet: In the U.S., Moderna withdrew its approval application for the combination vaccine in May last year and the timeline for resubmission remains uncertain.

items.title: Merck steps up as 'meaningful competitor' to Gilead with HIV pill approval
items.snippet: The newly approved HIV drug Idvynso will also help Merck diversify as loss of exclusivity looms over its top-selling product, the mega-blockbuster cancer drug Keytruda.

items.title: The 5 largest IPOs in biopharma history
items.snippet: With an IPO raise of $625 million, Kailera Therapeutics now holds the new record for the largest public market debut.

items.title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
items.snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

items.title: Replimune cries foul on regulatory flexibility. But many Americans want a stricter FDA
items.snippet: After Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug was rejected for a second time, CEO Sushil Patel slammed the FDA for failing to exercise regulatory flexibility, while other experts bemoaned the agency’s lack of consistency. With new safety guidelines for gene editing therapies, the FDA has taken a first step toward fixing bo...

items.title: With oral obesity race underway, all eyes are on Lilly’s Foundayo launch
items.snippet: Novo Nordisk’s oral Wegovy has a few months’ head start on Eli Lilly’s newly approved pill. While the Indianapolis pharma has come from behind the Danish rival in the weight loss space before, last time it clearly had the better drug.

items.title: Pharma's Reluctance To Serve Europe To Dodge MFN Drug Pricing Is Chilling
items.snippet: As Big Pharma companies consider foregoing European drug launches to avoid reducing drug prices in the U.S. in alignment with Trump’s Most Favored Nation policy, patients will suffer.
